subject: Public Health
title: Epidemiology in Climate and Health
description: A micro-lesson that makes Epidemiology in Climate and Health usable.

hook.text: Epidemiologists now track heat deaths, flood injuries, and expanding tropical diseases—climate epidemiology is here.

buildup.text: Climate change alters disease patterns—warmer temperatures expand mosquito habitats and increase heatwave deaths.

discovery.text: Time-series studies link daily temperature data to hospital admissions—showing direct health impacts of warming.

twist.text: Attribution science now quantifies how many deaths a specific heat wave caused—assigning a fraction to climate change.

climax.text: Climate-health epidemiology provides evidence for adaptation policies—cooling centers, early warnings, and urban.

punchline.text: The thermometer is the newest tool in the epidemiologist's kit.

quiz.question: What does attribution science do in climate epidemiology?
options: Quantifies how many deaths are caused by specific climate events
